name: San Sebastián de Garabandal desde Tudanca – circular por el embalse de La Cohilla
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tudanca offers a variety of routes through diverse terrain. The region is characterized by its hilly landscape, featuring significant elevation changes and natural water features like reservoirs and fountains. Trails often traverse forested areas and open mountain passes, providing varied riding experiences. The area's geology supports a network of paths suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Tudanca The most popular mountain bike trail is San Sebastián de Garabandal desde Tudanca – circular por el embalse de La Cohilla, a difficult 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features a loop around the La Cohilla reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Stone Fountain – Ucieda Parking with Fountain loop from Rionansa, a difficult 28.0 miles (45.1 km) path. This trail includes natural spring features and varied terrain near Rionansa. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Tojos – Correpoco Trail loop from Cabuérniga, a 32.5 miles (52.3 km) trail leading through the Cabuérniga valley, often completed in about 4 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Tudanca is defined by challenging ascents, forested sections, and routes around natural water bodies. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation gain. The routes in Tudanca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tudanca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Tudanca?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Tudanca, offering a variety of experiences through the region's hilly landscape, forested areas, and mountain passes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Tudanca?
A: While many routes in Tudanca feature significant elevation gain, there are several moderate trails that might be suitable for beginners looking for a challenge. Currently, there are 8 moderate trails available, with the remaining 15 being difficult. You can find options that traverse varied terrain without the most extreme ascents.

Q: What are some good mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000733340/san-sebastian-de-garabandal-desde-tudanca-circular-por-el-embalse-de-la-cohilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Sebastián de Garabandal desde Tudanca – circular por el embalse de La Cohilla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 25.1-mile (40.4 km) trail with over 1,300 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Fountain – Ucieda Parking with Fountain loop from Rionansa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 28.0 miles (45.1 km) and includes natural spring features.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Tudanca area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Tudanca are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000733340/san-sebastian-de-garabandal-desde-tudanca-circular-por-el-embalse-de-la-cohilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Sebastián de Garabandal desde Tudanca – circular por el embalse de La Cohilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Tojos – Correpoco Trail loop from Cabuérniga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Tudanca?
A: The region is rich in natural features. You can encounter significant elevation changes, forested areas, and natural water features like reservoirs and fountains. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5469498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo del Infierno Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/994831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiña Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by impressive summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Tudanca?
A: Given the hilly landscape and significant elevation changes, many trails offer panoramic views of the surrounding region. Routes that traverse open mountain passes, such as those leading to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;, are likely to provide excellent viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tudanca?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Tudanca is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the trails are less likely to be affected by extreme heat or winter conditions. The region's diverse terrain can be enjoyed in various seasons, but these periods offer optimal riding conditions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Tudanca?
A: The mountain bike trails in Tudanca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the challenging ascents, the varied terrain, and the scenic routes around natural water bodies like the La Cohilla reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by interesting historical or cultural landmarks?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, some routes may pass through or near traditional villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Parking with Fountain – Collada de Carmona loop from Cabuérniga&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Cabuérniga valley, an area known for its cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any trails that feature natural springs or fountains?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Parking with Fountain – Stone Fountain loop from Lamiña&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that highlights these natural springs and fountains.

Q: What is the typical length of mountain bike trails in Tudanca?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tudanca vary significantly in length. You can find routes ranging from moderate 15.8-mile (25.4 km) loops to more challenging 32.5-mile (52.3 km) expeditions. The average duration for these rides is typically between 2.5 to 4.5 hours, depending on the trail and your pace.
tips: The reservoir was built on the bed of the Nansa river and although it is not accessible, the stretch of road that borders it is very colorful and picturesque and will give you enough for some good photos in passing.

San Sebastián de Garabandal is a small mountain village in northern Spain in the Cantabrian Mountains.

There, from 1961 to 1965, four girls were to appear on June 18, 1961, first an angel, and on July 2, 1961, at the place designated by them, the Mother of God, as she was called, according to the girls, the angel. In the aftermath, there were supposedly many ecstasies and spectacular happenings, such as the lifting of gravity (the girls hovered above the ground) and even a previously announced miracle of the host, which was photographically captured.
